  "findings": [
    "Heat pump reversing valve (O/B terminal) is misconfigured in the thermostat settings, causing the system to heat when calling for cooling."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Access the installer setup menu on the thermostat.",
    "Reconfigure the O/B reversing valve setting to energize in the correct mode (typically 'O' for cooling for most brands like Rheem/Ruud, or 'B' for heating for brands like Bosch/Rheem depending on the outdoor unit specification)."
  ],
  "rationale": "The system is operating in reverse of the thermostat demand (heating during a call for cool). This is a classic configuration error where the reversing valve energization state (O vs B) does not match the physical heat pump chassis requirements, resulting in failed climate control sequence of operations.",
  "references": [
    "Manufacturer installation and operation manuals (thermostat installer setup guide)",
    "Manufacturer outdoor unit wiring and sequence of operations specifications"
  ]
}
